commit.message:
Run real vLLM instances in disaggregated serving demo

Launch two vLLM servers (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct) as Prefill and Decode,
route requests through the proxy, and send test prompts end-to-end.
Demonstrates the full Mooncake proxy pattern with real LLM inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add vLLM disaggregated serving proxy for real Mooncake + vLLM deployment

Implements a simplified version of Mooncake's vllm_v1_proxy_server.py that
routes requests between vLLM Prefill (kv_producer) and Decode (kv_consumer)
instances. Supports round-robin load balancing across multiple nodes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: nano-mooncake — Mooncake minimal prototype in ~650 lines of Python

Implements the core design of Mooncake (FAST 2025 Best Paper) as a teaching prototype:
- Transfer Engine with pluggable TCP transport and batch async transfers
- Mooncake Store with Master/Client, two-phase put, replica tracking, and lease eviction
- HTTP metadata server for segment discovery
- Two runnable examples: Prefill/Decode disaggregation and distributed KVCache store

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

through Axios on reconnect.

Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Implement news feed system with fanout architecture

Spring Boot 3.4 / Java 21 app with Nginx load balancer, Post Service
(PostgreSQL + Redis cache), Fanout Service (RabbitMQ → workers → News Feed
Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

commit.message:
Fixes #2398: Code Quality: Tighten exception handling in review pip... (#2436)

* Fixes #2398: Tighten exception handling in review pipeline

- reviewer.py: Add is_likely_bug() check in review(), fix_ci(), and
  fix_review_findings() to re-raise code bugs (TypeError, KeyError,
  AttributeError, etc.) instead of silently masking them as verdicts
- review_phase.py: Narrow 8 except-Exception blocks to catch only
  (RuntimeError, OSError) for subprocess/API failures, letting code
  bugs propagate
- pr_unsticker.py: Add is_likely_bug() re-raise in _process_item(),
  _resolve_ci_or_quality(), and _resolve_ci_timeout(); narrow 3 blocks
  to (RuntimeError, OSError) for subprocess/filesystem operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fixes #2398: Code Quality: Tighten exception handling in review pipeline

Auto-committed by HydraFlow (agent did not commit)

* Fixes #2398: Code Quality: Tighten exception handling in review pipeline

Auto-committed by HydraFlow (agent did not commit)

* review: fix ImportError not caught in _detect_language/_persist_troubleshooting_pattern, add OSError tests (PR #2436)

- Add ImportError to except tuple in _detect_language and
  _persist_troubleshooting_pattern so a missing module (ModuleNotFoundError)
  falls back gracefully instead of propagating.
- Add test for ModuleNotFoundError fallback in _detect_language.
- Add OSError tests for _fetch_code_scanning_alerts and
  _check_baseline_policy to cover the full narrowed catch tuple.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: add missing _resolve_ci_timeout is_likely_bug re-raise test (PR #2436)

The other two orchestration catch-alls (_process_item,
_resolve_ci_or_quality) both have tests verifying that code-bug
exceptions propagate. _resolve_ci_timeout had the same guard added
in this PR but no corresponding test — this adds it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace direct B2C URL fallback with Vercel retry strategy

Direct B2C URL was failing because of missing PKCE parameters.
Now retries portal load up to 3 times when Vercel blocks, waiting
for the JS challenge to auto-resolve.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: maintain positional alignment between drug names and enrichment fields

The previous aggregation used _append_unique() for per-drug scalar
fields (moa, drug_class, drug_name_normalized), which caused two
alignment bugs:
  1. Empty values were silently skipped — if Drug2 had no MoA, Drug1's
     MoA appeared to belong to Drug2 in the joined string
  2. Duplicate values were deduplicated — if two drugs shared the same
     MoA, only one entry was written but two drug names remained

Fix: per-drug scalar fields now always emit exactly one entry per drug
(empty string "" as a placeholder when data is missing), ensuring
index-for-index alignment with intervention_name after joining with "; ".

True list fields (molecular_targets, approved_indications) continue to
aggregate unique values across all drugs with no positional constraint.

fix: create tables in graph Initialize() + expand deploy diagnostics

CustomizationPlugin.UpdateDatabase() was never called during
publish. New approach: graph extension creates tables on first

Also expanded deploy.sh log filter to show ALL matching entries

commit.message:
feat: Add settings-config.json as source of truth for hook configuration

Created scripts/settings-config.json - the definitive hook settings file:
- Unified v5.2.1 hook configuration (nested matchers, Level 3.6 enforcement)
- Source of truth for all installations
- Version-controlled in the repo
- Setup scripts (both .sh and .ps1) now read from this file
- Eliminates hardcoded JSON in setup scripts

Changes:
1. Created scripts/settings-config.json with current unified hook config
2. Created scripts/README.md explaining settings management
3. Updated setup-global-claude.sh to read from settings-config.json
4. Updated setup-global-claude.ps1 to read from settings-config.json
5. Removed all hardcoded hook JSON from both setup scripts
6. Updated CLAUDE.md with settings configuration section

Benefits:
- Repos are source of truth (aligned with project philosophy)
- Settings are version-controlled and auditable
- Setup scripts are simpler and DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
- claude-code-ide can auto-sync from this repo
- Single edit point for all installations

Workflow:
1. Edit scripts/settings-config.json
2. Commit to repo
3. Run setup scripts (they read from config)
4. claude-code-ide syncs automatically
5. Never edit ~/.claude/settings.json directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: info column centered on mobile, right-third on desktop

Use .picker-info-column CSS class with media query: margin:0 auto
on mobile (centered), margin-left:auto margin-right:0 on desktop
(768px+) to push to the right third. maxWidth:340px constrains
the column width at both breakpoints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: normalize BRD compound IDs for metadata matching (4/1917 → full match)

Metadata broad_id uses long format (BRD-K12345678-001-01-1) while
morph/expr compound_id uses short format (BRD-K12345678). The exact
.map() match only found 4 hits, causing remove_controls to drop 1913
compounds with missing SMILES, leaving train=3, val=1, test=0.

Fix: extract the 13-char BRD prefix (BRD-[A-Za-z]\d{8}) from both
sides before matching. This is the standard normalization for Broad
Institute compound identifiers across CDRP and repurposing hub data.

Fix Date Night hours priority: check config.hours first for enabled flag

Individual day columns don't carry an enabled flag, so businesses
closed on a day could still have stale column values from onboarding.
config.hours has the enabled flag and is updated by the admin, so it
should be checked first. Also default to closed when no hours data
exists rather than assuming open.

commit.message:
fix: trimming-safe factory registration via assembly-level attribute, bump to v0.21.2

Replace trim-unsafe assembly.GetTypes() discovery with NeatooFactoryRegistrarAttribute.
All three factory types (class, static, interface) now emit
[assembly: NeatooFactoryRegistrar(typeof(X))], and RegisterFactories() enumerates
assembly attributes instead of scanning types. This fixes static and interface factories
being trimmed in Blazor WASM apps. Removes the v0.21.1 [DynamicDependency] workaround.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
